text.title: Diagnosing Low Activation and Prioritizing Experiments
text.summary: Symptom-to-cause mapping, ICE scoring for prioritization, and agent-specific gotchas.

list.item: Drop at signup step → friction: too many fields, no SSO, password requirements → reduce to email only, add magic link
Drop at onboarding → complexity: too many steps, no guidance → simplify to 3-5 steps, add templates
Drop before Aha moment → unclear next step → add tooltips, coach marks, activation checklist
Drop at core action → UX problems → user test and fix specific friction
Long time to activate → too many steps → remove non-essential steps, pre-fill defaults

section.title: ICE Scoring

rule.statement: Score every experiment hypothesis on Impact (1-10), Confidence (1-10), and Ease (1-10); prioritize by ICE average; run highest-scoring experiment first.
rule.rationale: ICE forces explicit judgment about ROI before investing engineering effort. Without scoring, teams default to easiest changes (high Ease, low Impact) rather than highest-value work.

section.reference: Python ICE scorer for ranking activation experiments

antipattern.description: Optimizing for high-completion events rather than retention-correlated behaviors.
antipattern.reason: Agents pick "completed onboarding" as activation because it has 80% completion vs "created a report" at 25%. But only the latter predicts retention. Always force the agent to score activation event candidates against D30 retention lift.

antipattern.description: Using "time-to-activation" without a fixed window definition.
antipattern.reason: Leaving the window open inflates activation rate and breaks week-over-week comparisons. Define window explicitly: e.g. "activated within 7 days of signup".

antipattern.description: Attacking the biggest funnel drop-off without confirming it is causal.
antipattern.reason: Funnel drop-offs are correlated with activation failure but not always the direct cause. Confirm with session recordings and user interviews before building the fix.

antipattern.description: Reducing signup friction before fixing downstream activation steps.
antipattern.reason: More signups with the same downstream funnel just inflates the top-of-funnel number. Fix activation rate before expanding acquisition.
